channel.title: +Wealth on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Wealth on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Foreign donations & spends
item.description:
Shia avadh

Awadh rulers funded projects such as the Hindiyya Canal in the 1780s-1790s with hundreds of thousands of rupees and provided ongoing annual contributions exceeding one million rupees at times through what became known as the Oudh Bequest.

Historical accounts confirm over six million rupees transferred from Awadh to Iraqi Shia sites between 1850 and 1903, including infrastructure and stipends to mujtahids, sourced from Awadh’s treasury and subjects.

Mecca & medina

Source: TW

item.title: Loot
item.description: Nadir Shah plundered so much from India that he canceled taxes in Iran for 3 years.
